repository.name: nanoclaw-todo-agent
repository.description: 🤖 智能待办 Agent skill for NanoClaw — AI-powered todo management via Telegram

commit.message:
feat: intelligent reminder lead time based on task type

- Meeting/appointment: remind 30 min before (not at start time)
- Payment/bills: remind 3 days before
- Deadline/submission: remind 1 day before
- Travel/flight: remind 2 hours before
- Medical appointment: remind 1 hour before
- Other timed tasks: remind 15 min before
- No time specified: remind at 9am on due date

Also: reminder confirmation always shows actual remind time vs due time

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix Odoo sync: add JSON-RPC session fallback for Odoo Online (SaaS)

that uses JSON-RPC session auth (/web/dataset/call_kw) with search_read.
Also added broader invoice/bill search fallback for different Odoo versions.
Debug logging added to track which method succeeds.

commit.message:
Add merge prompt when adding terms with existing source/target

When adding a term whose source or target already exists in the termbase
(but with a different translation), a dialog now offers to merge the new
text as a synonym instead of creating a near-duplicate entry. Works with
Alt+Down (quick-add), Alt+Up (project termbase), and Ctrl+Alt+T (dialog).

New files: TermMergeChecker.cs (match detection), MergePromptDialog.cs (UI).
New method: TermbaseReader.AddSynonym() for lightweight single-row insert.

Phase 4: Final README with real results and live app URL

- Live Streamlit badge + URL (appapppy-uzsmfcqvv5qdjsnewofujf.streamlit.app)
- Key results table: SARIMA 5.61% MAPE / RMSE 359 / MAE 272
- Business impact: annual costs from business_impact.csv (SARIMA $1.26M,
  LSTM $2.15M, Naive $1.17M); stockout-dominated cost structure explained
- Safety stock recommendation: 886 units @ 95% SL, 3-day lead time, $1,883/yr holding
- 5 EDA findings: weekly seasonality, SNAP lift, trend plateau, holiday anomalies, ADF test

  interactive calculator

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Revert Curchods slug parser — URLs are reference codes not descriptive slugs

Curchods URLs like /display/BWF250255 are opaque reference codes, not
human-readable slugs. The slug parser was turning these into bad addresses
like "BWF250255, Surrey". Reverted to using .property-card-town which
gives correct town names (Farnham, Alton, Hindhead, etc).

feat: SQLite schema, Zustand stores, CRUD operations

Wire all Zustand stores to SQLite via db query layer:
- taskStore: loadTasks(date), async addTask/updateTask/deleteTask/completeTask
- goalStore: loadGoals(weekStart), async addGoal/deleteGoal/updateProgress (clamped)
- rewardStore: loadRewards(weekStart), async addReward/claimReward/checkAndUnlock

Schema, migrations, and query files were complete in the scaffold.
Settings store already persisted via AsyncStorage (no changes needed).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Speed up SMS: use Haiku for tool-routing, Sonnet only for final response

When SMS requests require multiple tool calls (e.g. search_contacts →
send_email), intermediate API calls just need to decide which tool to
call next. Haiku handles this in ~1s vs Sonnet's 3-6s per call.

Flow: Sonnet (first call, full context) → Haiku (tool routing loops) →
Sonnet (final user-facing response, only if >80 chars). Short
confirmations like "Done!" stay as Haiku output.

Saves ~6-12s on multi-tool SMS requests like "email this to courtney".

commit.message:
Add README

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add GitHub Actions CI workflow and golangci-lint config (#5)

* Add GitHub Actions CI workflow and golangci-lint config

Set up a CI pipeline that runs on pull requests targeting master/main:
- Lint job using golangci-lint v2 with golangci-lint-action v9
- Test job with race detector across Go 1.24 and stable
- Minimal permissions (contents: read) on all jobs

The golangci-lint config enables errcheck, gosec, govet, ineffassign,
staticcheck and unused with test files excluded from linting.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01PkRcekSW1que7TkyqCmCuu

* Disable persist-credentials on checkout steps

Prevent the GitHub token from being exposed to subsequent steps
since the CI workflow does not need authenticated Git operations
after checkout.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01PkRcekSW1que7TkyqCmCuu

* Fix golangci-lint issues: variable shadow and struct field alignment

Rename shadowed err variable in cmd/mount.go and reorder struct fields
in client.go and control.go to minimize GC pointer scanning area.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01PkRcekSW1que7TkyqCmCuu

* Fix golangci-lint version format in CI workflow

The golangci-lint-action requires a version in v1.2 or v1.2.3 format,
not just the major version. Use v2.11 to get the latest patch release
in the v2.11.x line.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01PkRcekSW1que7TkyqCmCuu

* Quote golangci-lint version string in CI workflow

YAML can interpret unquoted values like v2.11 as a float, causing
the action to receive an invalid version. Quote it to ensure it is
passed as a string.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01PkRcekSW1que7TkyqCmCuu

* Remove unnecessary quotes around golangci-lint version

Values prefixed with a letter like v2.11 are already treated as
strings by YAML, so the quotes are not needed.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01PkRcekSW1que7TkyqCmCuu

* Add Go 1.25 to test matrix and CI badge to README

Go 1.25 and 1.26 are the two currently supported Go versions. Add
1.25 to the test matrix alongside 1.24 (project minimum) and stable
(currently 1.26). Also add a CI status badge to the README.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01PkRcekSW1que7TkyqCmCuu

---------
